result.summary: The page is mostly intact, but there is one clear data/content bug (a negative jackpot amount) plus several layout truncation issues where tiles and navigation labels are cut off at screen edges, which may confuse users.

issues.description: The 'Cop The Lot Megaways' game tile displays a negative jackpot value of '-$6,266.13', which is not a valid jackpot amount and suggests a data or calculation error.
issues.suggestion: Fix the jackpot feed/calculation so negative values are never displayed; add validation to hide or fall back to a default label if the value is invalid.

issues.description: The top navigation tab label 'Hot Gam...' is truncated at the right edge of the screen, making the category name unreadable.
issues.suggestion: If this is a scrollable carousel, add a visual affordance (gradient fade or partial-peek styling) or adjust tab widths so labels are not awkwardly cut mid-word.

issues.description: Game tiles in the right column ('Magnificent Phoenix', 'Munchie Millions', 'Hyperclusters', 'Dark Waters') are clipped by the viewport edge, showing only partial artwork and titles.
issues.suggestion: If these are horizontal carousels, ensure the peek amount is consistent and intentional; otherwise adjust the grid so full tiles fit or scroll indicators are shown.

issues.description: The bottom row of game tiles ('Who Wants To Be A Millionaire', pig-themed game) is partially obscured by the fixed bottom navigation bar.
issues.suggestion: Add bottom padding equal to the height of the fixed nav bar to the content container so the last row of tiles is fully visible when scrolled.

issues.description: The 'Casino' item in the bottom navigation is styled differently from the others (larger, underlined, lowercase serif-like styling) which looks inconsistent with the uniform uppercase labels of the other nav items.
issues.suggestion: Use a consistent active-state style (e.g., color highlight matching the other items' typography) rather than a different font size/case treatment.
